course_title: Goroutines, Concurrency & Parallelism in Go (Deep Dive)
assumptions: Basic understanding of functions and structs
Desire to write safe, high-performance concurrent systems
books_referenced: The Go Programming Language – Donovan & Kernighan

days.title: Why Goroutines Exist – Concurrency vs Parallelism
days.real_life_example: Web servers handle thousands of requests concurrently using goroutines without one thread per request.
theory_deep_dive: Go's philosophy: share memory by communicating
readings: Go Blog – 'Concurrency is Not Parallelism' (Rob Pike)

days.title: Goroutine Lifecycle & Scheduler Internals
days.real_life_example: High-QPS services rely on Go scheduler to multiplex millions of goroutines onto a few OS threads.

days.title: Memory Model & Happens-Before Guarantees
days.real_life_example: Incorrect memory visibility causes rare, unreproducible production bugs.
days.go_code:
var x int

go func() { x = 1 }()
fmt.Println(x)
days.hands_on_goal: Understand why this code is unsafe

days.title: Data Races & Race Detector
days.real_life_example: Race conditions often appear only under peak load.
theory_deep_dive: Why races are worse than crashes
days.go_code:
var counter int

go func() { counter++ }()
go func() { counter++ }()

days.title: Mutexes – Power and Danger
days.real_life_example: Incorrect mutex usage causes deadlocks or throughput collapse.
theory_deep_dive: When mutexes are the right tool
days.go_code:
var mu sync.Mutex

mu.Lock()
shared++
mu.Unlock()

days.title: RWMutex & Performance Trade-offs
days.real_life_example: Read-heavy caches benefit from RWMutex; write-heavy ones suffer.

days.title: Channels – The Core Abstraction
days.real_life_example: Pipelines use channels to safely pass data between stages.
days.go_code:
ch := make(chan int)

go func() { ch <- 42 }()
fmt.Println(<-ch)

days.title: Select, Timeouts & Cancellation
days.real_life_example: APIs must not wait forever on downstream dependencies.

days.title: Context Package – Structured Concurrency
days.go_code:
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Second)
defer cancel()

days.title: Worker Pools & Bounded Parallelism
Atomic Operations & Lock-Free Programming
days.real_life_example: Counters and metrics avoid locks using atomics.
days.hands_on_goal: Replace mutex with atomics where appropriate

days.title: Goroutine Leaks, Deadlocks & Debugging
days.real_life_example: Leaked goroutines cause memory bloat and CPU spikes.

days.title: Concurrency Patterns Used in Production
days.real_life_example: Rate limiters, pipelines, schedulers, fan-in systems.

days.title: Capstone – Concurrent System Design
days.real_life_example: Concurrent web crawler or task processor.
